summary: PR #2712 linked and matched the Old Yoshi stage TU by changing groldyoshi.c source shape, updating GALE01 symbol metadata, and flipping melee/gr/groldyoshi.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The bot report states two new matches: grOldYoshi_8020F31C reached 100% and .sbss reached 100%. Reviewer feedback only called out two unnecessary explanatory comments in the recovered function body.

key_files.role: Recovered source for grOldYoshi_8020F31C, introduced static inline helper grOldYoshi_8020F31C_inline, simplified stale comments in randi_between, and adjusted clamp-style return logic.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report new match grOldYoshi_8020F31C

key_files.role: Adjusted Old Yoshi symbol metadata, including local labels for strings/floats, size correction for grOy_803E650C and grOy_804D6A88, and local sdata/sdata2 entries.

what_changed.text: Linked groldyoshi by marking melee/gr/groldyoshi.c as Matching in configure.py.
Refactored grOldYoshi_8020F31C so part of the motion/distance adjustment lives in static inline grOldYoshi_8020F31C_inline, with a precomputed distance argument.
Removed stale/commented-out return code in randi_between and, after review, the diff evidence shows generated-style '// fVar2 = param1;' comments were targeted as unnecessary.
Updated Old Yoshi symbol definitions so several literals became local @NNN objects and corrected symbol sizes around .data, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt grOy_804DB9A8..C4 to local @ symbols

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded linked/matched progress: +232 matched code bytes, +3280 linked code bytes, and new 100% matches for grOldYoshi_8020F31C and groldyoshi .sbss.

smart_moves.text: Separated a repeated or codegen-sensitive subexpression path into a local static inline helper instead of keeping all logic in one large generated-looking function body.
Kept the helper local to the TU with static inline scope rather than exposing a new global symbol.
Paired source matching with symbol metadata updates and configure.py linkage, making the TU linkable as Matching rather than only changing C source.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When linking a stage TU, symbol metadata may need to change alongside source recovery; this PR converted several Old Yoshi literals/floats from global grOy_* names to local @NNN symbols and corrected sizes before marking the object Matching.
Reviewer-visible generated comments such as '// fVar2 = param1;' should be removed when they only document a temporary assignment and do not add source-level meaning.

naming_conventions.text: The PR retained address-style/generated names for unrecovered symbols and locals, including grOldYoshi_8020F31C, fVar2/fVar3/fVar4, and local @NNN symbol labels; no broad semantic rename is evidenced.
New helper name used the owning function name plus '_inline': grOldYoshi_8020F31C_inline.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a static inline helper to recover the matched shape for part of grOldYoshi_8020F31C while preserving a public wrapper function.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report grOldYoshi_8020F31C Before 79.74% After 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice does not include author explanation or objdiff details beyond the bot match report, so the exact codegen reason for the helper is inferred from the diff shape.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Precompute the distance expression in the wrapper and pass it into the inline helper, replacing an in-helper expression comparison with fVar1 < distance.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fVar4 computation and helper call
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No separate validation log is included beyond the bot report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Correct symbol scopes and sizes to support data/link matching, including changing several grOy_* literals/floats to local @NNN entries.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report linked data +328 bytes and .sbss 100%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice shows metadata changes but not the full symbol ownership rationale.

review_feedback.feedback: A second identical '// fVar2 = param1;' comment was also unnecessary.

follow_up_queries.reason: Find whether the helper shape became a recurring local matching pattern or remained unique to this PR.
Search for similar generated temporary-assignment comments that reviewers may consider cleanup noise.
Trace the .sbss correction associated with the bot-reported groldyoshi .sbss match.
Locate subsequent changes to the linked Old Yoshi TU.
Inspect symbol metadata history around local literal ownership for Old Yoshi.

evidence_refs: raw_diff: full diff for three changed files
text_corpus: bot report and review comments
notes: PR body is empty, so author intent is not directly documented.
The bot report provides match/link deltas, but no build log, objdiff screenshot, or commit discussion is included.
The review feedback is narrow and only supports removal of unnecessary comments; it does not prove broader reviewer intent.
The diff shows helper extraction and symbol metadata changes, but the exact assembly reason for each source-shape decision is not fully explained in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Remove generated temporary-assignment comments
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2712, reviewer ThePlayerRolo flagged two '// fVar2 = param1;' comments in grOldYoshi_8020F31C as unnecessary. Treat similar comments as cleanup candidates unless they carry real source-level meaning.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local static inline helper used in grOldYoshi_8020F31C match
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2712 matched grOldYoshi_8020F31C by splitting part of the logic into static inline grOldYoshi_8020F31C_inline and calling it from the public function after precomputing a distance expression.

source_update_candidates.title: Generated explanatory comments should not survive cleanup when they only restate assignments
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a concise cleanup note that comments copied from decompiler scratch work, such as '// fVar2 = param1;', should be removed when they do not explain source behavior. PR #2712 has direct reviewer feedback on this point.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards cover authored source shape and conservative naming, but do not explicitly mention generated scratch comments; curator can decide whether this is too small for a global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: groldyoshi.c was linked as Matching in PR #2712
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2712 changed configure.py to mark melee/gr/groldyoshi.c as Matching and the bot reported grOldYoshi_8020F31C and groldyoshi .sbss as new 100% matches.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is path-scoped historical context useful for future groldyoshi.c work; curator should verify against current source history before promoting.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that '_inline' helper extraction is always preferred; this PR only proves it was used here and the slice lacks detailed objdiff rationale.
Do not infer semantic meanings for fVar2/fVar3/fVar4 or grOy_* symbols; the PR retained generated/address-style naming.
Do not promote the symbol metadata changes as a global rule for all stage literals; evidence is specific to Old Yoshi addresses in this PR.
